data.title: The 10 Most Common Beginner Espresso Mistakes (and How to Fix Them)
data.author: Priya Sharma
data.intro: We field thousands of support messages a year, and the same issues come up again and again. None of them are about broken equipment — they are about small habits and misunderstandings that sabotage otherwise good shots. Here are the ten mistakes we see most often, with real fixes for each one.
data.excerpt: We field thousands of support messages a year, and the same issues come up again and again.

data.title: Blind Tasting Your Espresso: Evaluate Without Visual Cues
data.author: Marcus Webb
data.intro: We are all biased by what we see. A thick, tiger-striped crema convinces us a shot is good before it reaches our lips. A pale, thin extraction makes us wince before we taste. Blind tasting strips away those visual prejudices and forces you to evaluate espresso purely on flavor. It is the fastest way
data.excerpt: We are all biased by what we see.

data.title: Calibrating a Burr Grinder: Zero Point and Beyond
data.author: Sophie Chen
data.intro: Every burr grinder has a zero point — the setting where the burrs just barely touch. Finding and verifying this reference is the foundation of grinder calibration. If your zero point has drifted, every grind setting you use is slightly off. Here is how to recalibrate and why it matters more than you
data.excerpt: Every burr grinder has a zero point — the setting where the burrs just barely touch.

data.title: Descaling Your Espresso Machine: A Complete Step-by-Step Guide
data.author: Priya Sharma
data.intro: Scale buildup is the silent enemy of every espresso machine. Hard water deposits gradually coat boilers, thermoblocks, and internal tubing, choking flow rates and degrading temperature stability. Descaling takes less than thirty minutes, costs almost nothing, and can add years to your machine's life
data.excerpt: Scale buildup is the silent enemy of every espresso machine.

data.title: How to Clean a Group Head: The Backflush Routine You Should Know
data.author: Luca Bianchi
data.intro: The group head is where water meets coffee, and it accumulates oily residue faster than any other part of your machine. Left uncleaned, those oils go rancid and taint every shot you pull. Backflushing takes two minutes and keeps your espresso tasting clean. Here is how to do it properly.
data.excerpt: The group head is where water meets coffee, and it accumulates oily residue faster than any other part of your machine.

data.title: How to Dial In Espresso in Under 10 Minutes
data.author: Marcus Webb
data.intro: Dialing in a new bag of beans does not have to be a drawn-out, anxiety-inducing ritual that wastes half your coffee. With a structured approach and a willingness to taste honestly, you can land on a solid recipe in three to five shots — well under ten minutes. Here is the step-by-step method we teac
data.excerpt: Dialing in a new bag of beans does not have to be a drawn-out, anxiety-inducing ritual that wastes half your coffee.

data.title: How to Store Coffee Beans Properly: Containers, Freezing, and the Freshness Window
data.author: Elena Marchetti
data.intro: You can own the best grinder and machine on the market, but if your beans are stale, none of it matters. Coffee is a perishable product that degrades from the moment it is roasted. How you store it determines whether your Tuesday morning shot tastes as vibrant as your Monday one. Here is what actual
data.excerpt: You can own the best grinder and machine on the market, but if your beans are stale, none of it matters.

data.title: Pre-Infusion: What It Is and Why Arco Uses It
data.author: Sophie Chen
data.intro: Before the full force of nine bars of pressure hits your coffee puck, something quieter and arguably more important happens: pre-infusion. This brief, low-pressure wetting phase saturates the grounds evenly, seals micro-channels, and sets the stage for a more uniform extraction. Here is what pre-inf
data.excerpt: Before the full force of nine bars of pressure hits your coffee puck, something quieter and arguably more important happens: pre-infusion.

data.title: Replacing a Portafilter Gasket: When and How
data.author: Priya Sharma
data.intro: The group gasket is a small rubber ring that seals the connection between your portafilter and the group head. When it wears out, you get leaks, pressure loss, and inconsistent shots. Replacing it takes ten minutes, costs a few euros, and is one of the most satisfying maintenance tasks you can do at
data.excerpt: The group gasket is a small rubber ring that seals the connection between your portafilter and the group head.

data.title: Why Your Grinder Matters More Than Your Machine
data.author: Luca Bianchi
data.intro: Most people upgrading their home setup instinctively reach for a better espresso machine. That instinct is understandable but backwards. The single most impactful change you can make to the quality of your espresso is investing in a capable grinder, because grind quality dictates extraction more dir
data.excerpt: Most people upgrading their home setup instinctively reach for a better espresso machine.

data.title: Altitude and Espresso: The Science of Brewing at Elevation
data.author: Sophie Chen
data.intro: Water boils at 100 degrees Celsius at sea level. At 2,000 meters, it boils at 93 degrees. At 3,500 meters, it boils at 87 degrees. If you are brewing espresso at altitude — in a mountain cabin, on a ski trip, or while trekking — these numbers change everything about your extraction. Here is the phys
data.excerpt: Water boils at 100 degrees Celsius at sea level.

data.author: Sophie Chen
data.intro: Vienna's coffee house tradition is inscribed on the UNESCO Intangible Cultural Heritage list, and for good reason. data.excerpt: Vienna's coffee house tradition is inscribed on the UNESCO Intangible Cultural Heritage list, and for good reason.

data.excerpt: Lisbon has one of Europe's oldest coffee-drinking traditions and one of its newest specialty scenes.

data.intro: In Italy, dinner does not end with dessert. It ends with espresso. data.excerpt: The weekday morning shot is about speed and consistency.
